name: MRC (percent-FTP workout)
version: PerfPRO / TrainerRoad-era plain-text MRC course format.
spec: MRC is structurally identical to ERG but the data column is percentage of FTP rather than absolute watts. [COURSE HEADER] carries KEY = VALUE metadata with the legend 'MINUTES PERCENT'. [COURSE DATA] holds 'minutes percent' rows; percent is relative to the rider's FTP set in the trainer software, so one file scales across riders. Linear interpolation between rows; same-minute duplicate rows encode step changes. Because it is FTP-relative, MRC round-trips cleanly with ZWO and .ytw.

examples.title: Steady 65% for 20 min
examples.content:
[COURSE HEADER]
VERSION = 2
UNITS = ENGLISH
DESCRIPTION = Endurance
MINUTES PERCENT
[END COURSE HEADER]
[COURSE DATA]
0.00	65
20.00	65
[END COURSE DATA]

examples.title: 2x stepped sweet spot
examples.content:
[COURSE HEADER]
VERSION = 2
MINUTES PERCENT
[END COURSE HEADER]
[COURSE DATA]
0.00	50
5.00	50
5.00	90
25.00	90
25.00	50
30.00	50
[END COURSE DATA]

examples.title: Ramp 50->120%
examples.content:
[COURSE HEADER]
VERSION = 2
MINUTES PERCENT
[END COURSE HEADER]
[COURSE DATA]
0.00	50
20.00	120
[END COURSE DATA]

constraints.note: Percent is relative to FTP configured in the player software; the file itself stores no FTP.
Same-minute duplicate rows create instantaneous step transitions.

conversion_notes.note: Multiply each percent by FTP/100 to get absolute watts.
Divide percent by 100 for FTP fractions; group rows into steps (Warmup/SteadyState/Ramp/IntervalsT).

glossary.definition: Target power as a percentage of FTP (100 == FTP).
